title: Computer & Editor Tools

classes.name: Computer
properties: dimensions: tuple[int, int] | None (property, returns None)
abstract_methods: click(x: int, y: int, button: Button) -> None
scroll(x: int, y: int, scroll_x: int, scroll_y: int) -> None
classes.notes: Sync version. Subclasses back ComputerTool. Mouse methods may accept keyword-only 'keys' for modifier keys.

classes.name: AsyncComputer
properties: dimensions: tuple[int, int] | None (property, returns None)
abstract_methods: click(x: int, y: int, button: Button) -> Awaitable[None]
scroll(x: int, y: int, scroll_x: int, scroll_y: int) -> Awaitable[None]
classes.notes: Async mirror of Computer. All methods are coroutines.

classes.name: ApplyPatchOperation
classes.notes: Represents a single apply_patch editor operation requested by the model.

classes.name: ApplyPatchResult
classes.notes: Optional metadata returned by editor operations.

classes.name: ApplyPatchEditor
classes.notes: Host-defined editor protocol for applying diffs on disk. Supports sync and async via MaybeAwaitable.

classes.name: Prompt
classes.notes: Prompt configuration for interacting with an OpenAI model.

classes.name: GenerateDynamicPromptData
classes.notes: Input container passed to DynamicPromptFunction callables.

classes.name: PromptUtil
methods: to_model_input(prompt: Prompt | DynamicPromptFunction | None, context: RunContextWrapper[Any], agent: Agent[Any]) -> Awaitable[ResponsePromptParam | None]  (async, static)
classes.notes: Resolves static or dynamic prompts to OpenAI ResponsePromptParam. Calls dynamic function with GenerateDynamicPromptData, awaits if coroutine.

key_functions: apply_diff(input: str, diff: str, mode: ApplyDiffMode = 'default') -> str  — public entry point in apply_diff.py
_parse_update_diff(lines, input) -> ParsedUpdateDiff — parses context-hunk update diffs, returns Chunk list
_apply_chunks(input, chunks, newline) -> str — applies Chunk edits to source text
_find_context(lines, context, start, eof) -> ContextMatch — fuzzy context search with 3-tier fuzz (exact/rstrip/strip)
_read_section(lines, start_index) -> ReadSectionResult — reads one diff section into Chunks
key_relationships: AsyncComputer mirrors Computer — identical interface but all methods are async coroutines
ApplyPatchEditor uses ApplyPatchOperation (parameter) and returns ApplyPatchResult
PromptUtil depends_on GenerateDynamicPromptData (constructs it for dynamic dispatch)
apply_diff() uses ParserState, Chunk, ParsedUpdateDiff, ReadSectionResult, ContextMatch internally
external_dependencies: agents.exceptions.UserError — raised by PromptUtil.to_model_input on bad dynamic prompt
important_notes: Computer and AsyncComputer are parallel abstract hierarchies (sync/async) intended as user-subclassed backends for ComputerTool. ApplyPatchEditor is a @runtime_checkable Protocol — implementations can be duck-typed without explicit inheritance. apply_diff uses a V4A diff format with '@@' anchors, '+'/'-'/' ' line prefixes, and fuzzy context matching (3 tiers of whitespace tolerance tracked in fuzz score). Prompt/PromptUtil bridge static TypedDict prompts and DynamicPromptFunction callables to OpenAI's ResponsePromptParam.
